and Japan so we continue to see European

we had uh the headline CPI flash PMI or
CBI uh for the month of October
accelerate 70 basis points to 10 support
seven percent that's an all-time high we
had uh the core CPI for the Eurozone
accelerate 20 basis points to five
percent that's also an all-time high uh
remember the ECB has an official
officially has a singular mandate uh to
sort of maintain two percent inflation
um it obviously has the price stability
mandate or sorry the uh Financial
stability mandate as well as it relates
to European spreads and that's really
creating sort of a challenge for the ECB
as it relates to being able to really
defend its currency uh and really bring
down inflation we saw some pretty dovish
commentary out of the Guard uh on last
Thursday at the ECB meeting uh and then
shifting gears to China uh continue to
see just signs of liquidity liquidity
it's just they're tightening liquidity
to defend the rim and B while they're
also in a liquidity trap from a from a
secular standpoint and then you lay
around the fact that zero covet
continues to be an issue Weighing on
growth broadly it's just an awful policy
mix there um you just get in the
official manufacturing data official PMI
data for the month of October uh
manufacturing tick down 0.9 points to
49.2 that's the lowest we've seen in
three months and then the services
ticked down 1.9 points to
48.48.7 that's the lowest we've seen in
five months so these are really rancid
numbers showing that the Chinese economy
continues to contract at the margins and
then lastly Japan that's been a a great
move talk about this all Japan for most
of the Year saying hey look if you're a
long only mutual fund style investor

certainly one of those places uh
particularly within developed Market

relative performance basis relative to
the rest of the available menu and that
continues to be supported by Japan's uh
growth obviously Japan's policy has been
very supportive for stocks but certainly
the growth there has been quite
supportive as well so for the month of
September we had uh Japanese industrial
production accelerate 400 basis points
to 9.8 percent year over year that's the
fastest rate we've seen since July of
2021 and then retail sales accelerate 40
basis points to force about five percent
year over year in September that size
we've seen since May at 21 and so

big Divergence in European inflation
relative to places like China big

to a place like China and all these

um you know it's best to sort of reduce

are and ultimately how they're going to
ask impact asset markets on the median

you don't need to be in hurry to trade
every single wiggle in the equity at
crypto markets et cetera et cetera what
you want to do is make sure you're
achieving your medium to longer term
investment objectives and we'll figure
out what those investment objectives are
and Orient your risk management
according to that if your investment
object it was our trade every wiggle in
